picks.title: PUBG Ally: an NVIDIA ACE-powered, co-playable AI teammate (2-week beta)
picks.excerpt: Krafton and NVIDIA have opened a limited-time beta letting PUBG players team up in duos with 'PUBG Ally', a co-playable AI teammate powered by NVIDIA ACE and an on-device small language model that talks, finds loot, drives, and fights alongside you.
picks.rationale: Concrete, shippable AI-teammate beta (on-device SLM + NVIDIA ACE) that shows how gen-AI agents enter live gameplay, not just tooling.

picks.title: What's new in MetaHuman in Unreal Engine 5.8
picks.excerpt: Unreal Engine 5.8's MetaHuman update adds an experimental Collections crowds system and AI-driven markerless motion capture (via Meshcapade) that turns single-camera video into full-body and facial performance, scaling to thousands of characters.
picks.rationale: Actionable pipeline news: markerless single-camera mocap and crowd Collections change how character animators and worldbuilders work today.

picks.title: Why so many game developers don't want to use generative AI
picks.excerpt: A feature digging into why many game developers remain reluctant to adopt generative AI in their craft, weighing workflow, authorship, quality, and craft concerns against studio pressure to embrace the tools.
picks.rationale: Insight piece that frames the craft/authorship pushback shaping which AI tools studios actually adopt, complementing the tool releases with the human counterweight.

picks.title: General Intuition's $2.3B bet that video games can train AI agents for the real world
picks.excerpt: General Intuition is building world models and agentic models trained on video-game gameplay data, betting that the action and causality embedded in games can teach AI agents spatial reasoning that transfers from Fortnite-style worlds to robotics and the real world.
picks.rationale: Fresh, credible TechCrunch scoop on a major funding round reframing gameplay data as training fuel for embodied AI agents - novel and forward-looking.

dropped.reason: Dedup backstop: this is the same PC Gamer Tim Sweeney interview already published 2026-06-26 in sections.games as the Wccftech card 'Sweeney: Steam AI disclosure is irresponsible' (wccftech.com/valve-irresponsible-ai-disclosures-steam-epic-ceo-unreal-engine-6/), which is explicitly built on this interview. Same event, different outlet = duplicate.

shortfall_note: User selected 5; one (candidate #3) dropped as a duplicate of an already-published story. 4 distinct fresh picks remain. No substitution/padding per rules.
